title: Meeting at the Supermarket
description: Learn basic greetings, introductions, and how to ask for fruit at a Dutch supermarket.

content.text: Welcome to your first Dutch lesson! You will learn everything you need for a trip to a Dutch supermarket ('de supermarkt'). By the end, you will be able to greet people, introduce yourself, and buy fruit.

content.title: Dutch Articles: 'de' and 'het'
content.explanation:
Dutch has two words for 'the': 'de' and 'het'. Most nouns use 'de' (about two-thirds of all nouns). You need to memorize which nouns use 'het'. For now, just pay attention to which article each new word uses.

Example: 'de supermarkt' (the supermarket) uses 'de'.

content.title: Quick Practice: Introduce Yourself
content.bot_line: Hallo! Ik heet Jan. Hoe heet jij?
content.expected_concept: The user should greet back with 'Hallo' and introduce themselves using 'Ik heet' followed by their name

content.title: Meeting Someone New
content.setting: You are in the supermarket and meet a friendly Dutch person named Alex. Introduce yourself!

conversation_flow.title: Say your name using 'Ik heet...'
conversation_flow.chatbot_message: Ik heet Alex. Hoe heet jij?

extract_info.user_name: Extract the user's name from their message

content.text: Great job with your first Dutch conversation! Now let us learn some vocabulary for shopping.
Notice that 'een' means both 'a/an' and the number 'one' in Dutch. 'Ik wil graag een appel' means 'I would like an apple.'

content.title: Quick Practice: Ask for Fruit
content.expected_concept: The user should greet back and ask for any fruit using 'Ik wil graag een...' followed by a fruit name (appel, banaan, or sinaasappel)

content.title: Buying Fruit
content.setting: You walk to the fruit section of the supermarket. A friendly employee greets you.

conversation_flow.title: Greet and ask for an apple using 'Ik wil graag...'

content.text: Excellent! You can now greet someone, introduce yourself, and buy fruit in Dutch. In the next lesson, we will visit a famous Dutch fish stand, 'de haringkraam'!
